extraction.article_title: Marimo RCE Flaw CVE-2026-39987 Exploited Within 10 Hours of Disclosure

ttps.description: Exploit Public-Facing Application via an unauthenticated WebSocket terminal endpoint that can be reached over the network and used to obtain an interactive shell.
Command and Scripting Interpreter through arbitrary command execution in an interactive PTY shell.
Data from Local System by using the shell to search for sensitive files such as .env and SSH keys.

attack_chain: Search for secrets (.env, SSH keys, credentials)
Exfiltrate sensitive data or reuse credentials for follow-on access

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: An exposed WebSocket terminal endpoint accepts an upgrade request without enforcing authentication, allowing the attacker to establish a shell session directly. The test checks whether the endpoint returns a successful WebSocket upgrade and whether terminal I/O is accepted without any prior login or token.
proposed_kb_patterns.payload:
GET /terminal/ws HTTP/1.1
Host: target.example
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Key: dGhlIHNhbXBsZSBub25jZQ==
Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13
Origin: http://target.example

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: Once the terminal is obtained, arbitrary commands can be issued to the underlying PTY. A safe validation payload should confirm command execution by requesting a benign system identifier and looking for the expected output in the terminal stream.

With shell access, the attacker can traverse the filesystem to locate application secrets and environment files. The goal is to verify whether sensitive configuration files are readable from the running context.
detect: permission errors mixed with readable secrets

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: If the terminal or notebook environment can invoke network clients or Python libraries, an attacker may coerce the server to make outbound requests and observe internal network reachability. A simple loopback fetch helps determine whether server-side request capability exists.
detect: HTTP response from loopback or internal service
